ui.adminRestart: ⚠ Please restart the app as Administrator
ui.adminHintSidebar: Some tweaks need admin. Restart as administrator to unlock them.
ui.bootSub: Boot times from Event Log (ID 100) · Read/write BCD settings · Last 20 boots as chart
ui.bootTweaksNote: All changes are made via bcdedit. Takes effect on next reboot. Can be reverted at any time.
ui.bootNoData: ⏻ Boot times will appear here after the next reboot.
ui.bootLogDisabled: The boot performance log was disabled and has just been enabled.
ui.bootLogEmpty: The boot performance log is enabled but contains no entries yet.
ui.bootRestartHint: Just restart — all future boots will be recorded here.
ui.bootAdminHint: ⚠ Run as Administrator to modify BCD settings.
ui.privSub: Windows telemetry, ad ID, activity history, location & feedback — all in one place. All changes are reversible.
ui.svcSub: Disabling bloat services saves RAM and reduces background activity. Services can be re-enabled at any time.
ui.svcAdminWarn: ⚠ Administrator rights required to control services.
ui.healthSub: SFC and DISM check and repair Windows system files and the component store. All checks require Administrator rights.
ui.healthAdminWarn: ⚠ Please start the app as Administrator to run health checks.
ui.healthCorrupt: ⚠ Corrupt files found — run DISM RestoreHealth.
ui.sfcDesc: Checks and repairs protected Windows system files.
ui.dismCheckDesc: Quick check of the component store (no network, ~30 sec).
ui.dismScanDesc: Full local scan of the component store (~5 min).
ui.dismRestoreDesc: Repairs the component store via Windows Update (internet required).
ui.dismComponentDesc: Removes outdated components and frees disk space.
ui.hw3: If SFC reports errors → run DISM RestoreHealth (needs internet)
ui.hw4: After restore → run SFC again to confirm the repair
ui.hw5: Optional: ComponentCleanup to free disk space
ui.updatesSub: Apps via winget · Drivers via Windows Update (WHQL-signed only) · No third-party downloads
ui.updatesAllCurrent: All apps are up to date ✔
ui.updatesAdminHint: ⚠ Some system apps require admin rights to update
ui.updatesDriverConfirm: Recommendation: create a restore point first.
ui.updatesGpuTitle: GPU Drivers — directly from the manufacturer
ui.updatesGpuNote: Windows Update GPU drivers are months out of date. For
ui.diskSub: Largest files & folders · Duplicate detection (SHA-256) · Temp file age breakdown
ui.startupSub: Enable/disable startup entries exactly like Task Manager (StartupApproved) — nothing is deleted, every toggle is instantly reversible. Fewer startup items = faster boot + less idle RAM.
ui.startupLoading: Reading Run keys and startup folders…
ui.startupTip: Tip: Disable instead of delete — takes effect on next restart. Check unknown entries on the Security page first.
ui.procSub: Terminate, set priority (instant or permanent) and CPU affinity. Critical system processes are protected on the backend.
ui.procRealTimeWarn: RealTime can freeze the system. Sure?
ui.procPermSuffix: (applies to every future start, via IFEO — admin required):
ui.gpuSub: Driver-level registry optimizations for the detected GPU. All changes are directly reversible. NVIDIA: PowerMizer, P-States, Telemetry. AMD: ULPS, Shader-Sleep, Compute-Preemption.
ui.gpuNoSupport: ⚠ No supported discrete GPU detected. GPU-specific tweaks are not available.
ui.gpuNoKeyDesc: ⚠ Driver registry key not found. GPU may not be detected or driver not installed.
ui.gpuNeedsAdmin: ⚠ This tweak requires admin rights (medium-risk). Start the app as Administrator.
ui.schedSub: Windows Scheduled Tasks — many Microsoft internal tasks collect telemetry or run pointlessly in the background. Disabling stops them without deleting (re-enable anytime).
ui.schedAdminWarn: ⚠ Admin rights required to disable tasks. Start the app as Administrator.
ui.schedTip: Tip: Only disable known bloatware — leave your own or app-specific tasks (e.g. antivirus, backup) alone.
ui.regAdminHint: ⚠ HKLM entries (Shell Extensions, App Paths, SharedDLLs) require admin rights.
ui.regBackupNote: Backup written before action · can be restored at any time
ui.regEmpty: ✓ No orphaned entries found — Registry is clean.
ui.secInstallerNote: Note: Installers (setup.exe) legitimately run from Temp while you're installing something. Unknown? First "Check online", then Quick-Scan.
ui.secLighspeedNote: Note: "LIGHTSPEED Receiver" is the Logitech wireless driver — known unsigned, harmless.
ui.profilesSub: Curated tweak bundles in one click — restore point beforehand, optional auto-benchmark before/after. Every individual tweak remains separately reversible via the journal.
ui.profilesRunningBench: Benchmark → Apply tweaks → Benchmark. CPU/RAM/Disk are measured — don't run anything heavy in the meantime.

power_high_performance.name: High Performance power plan
power_high_performance.description: Switches the active power plan to High Performance.
power_high_performance.rationale: Balanced plan downclocks cores aggressively; High Performance keeps clocks ready, reducing latency spikes in games and interactive workloads.
power_high_performance.impact: Lower input/scheduling latency; higher idle power draw (relevant on laptops).

game_dvr_off.name: Disable Game DVR / background recording
game_dvr_off.description: Turns off Xbox Game DVR background capture.
game_dvr_off.rationale: Background capture continuously encodes frames, costing GPU/CPU time and occasionally introducing frametime spikes.
game_dvr_off.impact: Measurable frametime stability gain on weaker GPUs.

mmcss_gaming.name: MMCSS gaming scheduler profile
mmcss_gaming.description: Sets SystemResponsiveness=10 and disables network throttling (NetworkThrottlingIndex=0xFFFFFFFF).
mmcss_gaming.rationale: MMCSS reserves 20% CPU for background tasks by default and throttles network packets during multimedia playback; both are conservative for gaming rigs.
mmcss_gaming.impact: Smoother frametimes under load; slightly less CPU reserved for background services.

startup_delay_off.name: Remove artificial startup app delay
startup_delay_off.rationale: Windows staggers startup apps by several seconds after login; removing the delay makes the desktop usable sooner.

visual_fx_performance.name: Visual effects: best performance
visual_fx_performance.description: Sets the visual-effects preference to 'Adjust for best performance'.
visual_fx_performance.rationale: Animations and transparency cost GPU/CPU time and RAM, most noticeable on iGPUs and low-RAM systems.
visual_fx_performance.impact: Snappier UI on low-end hardware; cosmetic downgrade.

telemetry_minimal.name: Minimize telemetry (policy + DiagTrack)
telemetry_minimal.description: Sets AllowTelemetry policy to Security(0)/Basic and disables the Connected User Experiences (DiagTrack) service.
telemetry_minimal.rationale: Reduces background data collection, disk writes and network chatter from the telemetry pipeline.
telemetry_minimal.impact: Less background I/O; some diagnostic data unavailable to Microsoft support.

advertising_id_off.name: Disable advertising ID
advertising_id_off.description: Turns off the per-user advertising identifier.
advertising_id_off.rationale: Stops apps from using your advertising ID for cross-app profiling. Zero performance cost, pure privacy win.

sysmain_off.name: Disable SysMain/Superfetch (SSD systems)
sysmain_off.description: Sets the SysMain service to Disabled.
sysmain_off.rationale: SysMain's prefetching helps HDDs; on SSDs it adds background I/O and RAM pressure with negligible benefit. Only apply if your system drive is an SSD.
sysmain_off.impact: Less background disk activity on SSDs. Do NOT apply on HDD systems.

hibernate_off.name: Disable hibernation (free hiberfil.sys)
hibernate_off.rationale: hiberfil.sys reserves ~40% of RAM size on disk. Desktops that never hibernate get that space back. Also disables Fast Startup.
hibernate_off.impact: Frees several GB on the system drive; hibernate/Fast Startup unavailable until reverted.

menu_delay_fast.name: Faster menu response
menu_delay_fast.rationale: Pure artificial delay before menus open; lowering it makes the shell feel faster at zero cost. (Don't set 0 — hover menus become twitchy.)

content_suggestions_off.name: Disable tips, suggestions & Start ads
content_suggestions_off.description: Turns off Content Delivery Manager suggestion surfaces.
content_suggestions_off.rationale: Suggestion content is fetched and rendered in the background and occasionally installs sponsored apps.
content_suggestions_off.impact: Cleaner Start menu, less background fetching.

background_apps_off.name: Disable UWP background apps (global)
background_apps_off.description: Prevents Store apps from running in the background for this user.
background_apps_off.rationale: Each background UWP app costs RAM and wake-ups. Apps still work when opened; they just can't idle in the background.
background_apps_off.impact: Lower idle RAM/CPU. Live tiles and some notifications stop updating.

mouse_accel_off.name: Disable mouse acceleration (Enhance Pointer Precision)
mouse_accel_off.rationale: Acceleration makes cursor distance depend on speed — bad for aim consistency. Esports-standard tweak.
mouse_accel_off.impact: 1:1 mouse input. Cursor feel changes; takes adjustment.

game_mode_on.name: Enable Windows Game Mode
game_mode_on.description: Turns on Game Mode (auto-prioritizes the game, defers Windows Update scans while playing).
game_mode_on.rationale: Game Mode steers scheduler attention to the foreground game and suppresses update activity during play. Microsoft's own supported gaming optimization.

fse_optimizations_off.name: Disable Fullscreen Optimizations (FSE behavior)
fse_optimizations_off.description: Forces classic exclusive-fullscreen behavior instead of Windows' borderless-fullscreen wrapper.
fse_optimizations_off.rationale: Fullscreen Optimizations route games through DWM composition. On many systems classic exclusive fullscreen gives lower input latency and steadier frametimes.
fse_optimizations_off.impact: Lower input lag in fullscreen games on affected systems; Alt-Tab becomes slower.

hags_on.name: Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling (HAGS)
hags_on.description: Sets HwSchMode=2, letting the GPU manage its own scheduling queue. REBOOT REQUIRED.
hags_on.rationale: Offloads frame scheduling from CPU to a dedicated GPU scheduler — reduces latency on modern GPUs (NVIDIA 10-series+/AMD RDNA+), and is required for DLSS 3 Frame Generation.
hags_on.impact: Slightly lower latency on modern GPUs. Requires reboot. On very old GPUs/drivers it can hurt — benchmark before/after.

priority_separation.name: Foreground priority boost (Win32PrioritySeparation 0x26)
priority_separation.description: Sets scheduler quanta to short, fixed, 3:1 foreground boost.
priority_separation.rationale: Default Windows gives the foreground app a variable boost; 0x26 makes quanta short and fixed with maximum foreground bias — the classic esports scheduler tweak, keeping the game responsive when background tasks fire.
priority_separation.impact: More consistent foreground frametimes under background load.

power_throttling_off.name: Disable Power Throttling (EcoQoS)
power_throttling_off.description: Globally disables Windows power throttling of background processes. REBOOT RECOMMENDED.
power_throttling_off.rationale: EcoQoS forces processes onto efficiency states/E-cores; it sometimes misclassifies game launchers, overlays, OBS or anti-cheat helpers, starving them mid-game.
power_throttling_off.impact: No process gets force-throttled. Higher idle power draw — skip on laptops running on battery.

mmcss_games_task.name: MMCSS 'Games' task: maximum priority
mmcss_games_task.description: Raises the multimedia scheduler's Games profile: GPU Priority 8, CPU Priority 6, Scheduling Category High, SFIO High.
mmcss_games_task.rationale: Games registering with MMCSS (most DirectX titles) inherit this profile; the defaults (GPU Priority 2, Priority 2, Category Medium) are conservative.
mmcss_games_task.impact: Higher scheduling priority for games that use MMCSS.

mpo_off.name: Disable Multiplane Overlay (MPO)
mpo_off.description: Sets DWM OverlayTestMode=5, disabling multiplane overlays. REBOOT REQUIRED.
mpo_off.rationale: MPO causes stutter, flicker and black-screen glitches on a range of GPU/monitor combos — both NVIDIA and AMD have historically recommended this exact toggle as the workaround.
mpo_off.impact: Fixes MPO-related flicker/stutter if present. Slightly higher DWM GPU load. Only apply if you see flicker/stutter; undo if no change.

gamebar_popups_off.name: Disable Game Bar Popups & Start Panel
gamebar_popups_off.description: Disables Game Bar start panel and Nexus overlay hints.
gamebar_popups_off.rationale: Game Bar popups interrupt games and cost focus/frames on startup.
gamebar_popups_off.impact: No more Game Bar popups; Win+G still works manually.

transparency_off.name: Disable Transparency Effects
transparency_off.description: Disables acrylic/blur transparency in taskbar, Start menu, and windows.
transparency_off.rationale: Blur effects permanently consume GPU time (DWM) — measurable on iGPUs and weaker cards.
transparency_off.impact: Lower DWM GPU load; UI looks more plain.

min_animate_off.name: Disable Window Minimize Animation
min_animate_off.rationale: Pure waiting animation (~200ms per action). Without it, window handling feels instant.

start_websearch_off.name: Disable Web Search in Start Menu
start_websearch_off.description: Start menu search shows only local results (no Bing suggestions).
start_websearch_off.rationale: Every keystroke in Start search otherwise goes to Bing — adds latency and data leakage.
start_websearch_off.impact: Faster, purely local Start menu search.

lockscreen_ads_off.name: Disable Lock Screen Spotlight / Ads
lockscreen_ads_off.description: Disables Windows Spotlight overlays and Fun Facts on the lock screen.
lockscreen_ads_off.rationale: Spotlight downloads content in the background and shows sponsored hints.
lockscreen_ads_off.impact: Static lock screen, no background fetching.

widgets_off.name: Disable Widgets / News & Interests (Policy)
widgets_off.description: Disables the Widget Board (Win11) via policy.
widgets_off.rationale: The widget process (msedgewebview2) runs permanently, fetches news, and costs RAM + network.
widgets_off.impact: Permanently saves RAM and background traffic. Widget button disappears.

delivery_opt_off.name: Disable Update P2P (Delivery Optimization)
delivery_opt_off.description: Sets DODownloadMode=0 — updates come only from Microsoft, no peer-to-peer.
delivery_opt_off.rationale: Windows otherwise uploads updates to other PCs (upload bandwidth!) and keeps a disk cache.
delivery_opt_off.impact: No upload consumption from Windows Update; slightly slower update downloads possible.

wer_off.name: Disable Windows Error Reporting
wer_off.description: Disables automatic crash report collection and upload.
wer_off.rationale: WER writes crash dumps to disk and uploads them — I/O spikes exactly when the system is already struggling.
wer_off.impact: No more WER dumps/uploads. Downside: less crash diagnostic data if you debug yourself.

activity_history_off.name: Disable Activity History (Timeline)
activity_history_off.rationale: Every opened app/file is otherwise written to the activity database — background I/O and data collection.
activity_history_off.impact: Fewer background writes; Win+Tab Timeline stays empty.

usb_suspend_off.name: Disable USB Selective Suspend
usb_suspend_off.description: Prevents Windows from putting USB devices to sleep during use.
usb_suspend_off.rationale: Selective Suspend causes USB audio dropouts, micro-stutters with mice/headsets, and controller reconnects.
usb_suspend_off.impact: Stable USB devices; slightly higher power draw (negligible on desktop, noticeable on laptop battery).

fast_startup_off.name: Disable Fast Startup
fast_startup_off.description: Disables hiberboot — Shutdown performs a full cold shutdown.
fast_startup_off.rationale: Fast Startup freezes the kernel instead of restarting: driver issues survive reboots, dual-boot setups get corrupted, uptime grows indefinitely. A real cold boot fixes more than it costs.
fast_startup_off.impact: Boot ~5–15s slower, but a fresh kernel state every time. Hibernate itself remains available.

disable_paging_executive.name: Keep Kernel in RAM (DisablePagingExecutive)
disable_paging_executive.description: Prevents kernel code and drivers from being paged out to the swap file.
disable_paging_executive.rationale: By default Windows can write kernel pages to disk. With ≥8 GB RAM this only adds latency when kernel code is read back — disabling keeps everything in RAM.
disable_paging_executive.impact: Lower kernel call latency; marginally higher RAM usage (~50 MB). Do not apply on systems with less than 8 GB RAM.

core_parking_off.name: Disable CPU Core Parking
core_parking_off.description: Keeps all CPU cores permanently active (Core Parking Min Cores = 100%). REBOOT RECOMMENDED.
core_parking_off.rationale: Windows parks cores at idle to save power. Waking parked cores takes a few milliseconds — visible as frametime spikes on first load increase in a game.
core_parking_off.impact: Lower startup frametimes and fewer latency spikes; slightly higher idle power draw (negligible on desktop, noticeable on laptop battery).

ntfs_last_access_off.name: Disable NTFS Last-Access Timestamp
ntfs_last_access_off.description: Prevents NTFS from writing the last-accessed timestamp on every read.
ntfs_last_access_off.rationale: Every read otherwise triggers an MFT write. On heavily-read SSDs this adds up to unnecessary write amplification and latency spikes.
ntfs_last_access_off.impact: Significantly fewer background writes on all NTFS volumes. Pure performance gain on SSDs.

ntfs_8dot3_off.name: Disable 8.3 Short Names (DOS compatibility)
ntfs_8dot3_off.description: Stops automatic generation of 8.3 short names (e.g. PROGRA~1) for new files.
ntfs_8dot3_off.rationale: On every file creation NTFS checks if an 8.3 short name must be generated — unnecessary overhead; no modern program needs this legacy compatibility.
ntfs_8dot3_off.impact: Faster file creation in large directories; no 8.3 names for newly created files.

xbox_services_off.name: Set Xbox Background Services to Manual
xbox_services_off.description: Sets XblAuthManager, XblGameSave, and XboxNetApiSvc to Manual.
xbox_services_off.rationale: These services run permanently but are only needed for Xbox Live multiplayer and Game Pass cloud sync — most PC gamers without an Xbox account never use them.
xbox_services_off.impact: Less idle RAM and service overhead. Xbox Live auth and cloud saves must be set back to Automatic if needed.

remote_registry_off.name: Disable Remote Registry
remote_registry_off.description: Sets the RemoteRegistry service to Disabled.
remote_registry_off.rationale: Allows external programs to access the registry over the network — a known attack vector that home users never need.
remote_registry_off.impact: Improved security; no more remote registry access over the network.

cortana_off.name: Disable Cortana (Policy)
cortana_off.rationale: Cortana runs as a background process, sends voice metrics, and permanently uses RAM — no value for most users.
cortana_off.impact: No more Cortana process; less data transfer to Microsoft.

edge_prelaunch_off.name: Disable Edge Preloading (Startup Boost)
edge_prelaunch_off.description: Prevents Edge from silently preloading in the background on Windows startup.
edge_prelaunch_off.rationale: Edge auto-starts in the background on boot to open faster on first use — costs RAM even when Edge is never used.
edge_prelaunch_off.impact: Less RAM usage after boot; Edge opens slightly slower on first launch.

search_highlights_off.name: Disable Search Highlights & Trending Content
search_highlights_off.description: Disables dynamic news and trending content in the Windows search box.
search_highlights_off.rationale: The search box downloads new highlight content daily and shows it on open — background traffic and distraction with no value.
search_highlights_off.impact: No network fetching by the search box; calmer, faster search box.

auto_play_off.name: Disable AutoPlay
auto_play_off.description: Prevents Windows from auto-running USB drives or media when plugged in.
auto_play_off.rationale: AutoPlay is a classic attack vector for autorun malware. No modern system needs auto-starting unknown devices.
auto_play_off.impact: Security improvement; USB devices and disc media no longer open automatically.

svc_fax_off.name: Disable Fax Service
svc_fax_off.description: Sets the Fax service to Disabled.
svc_fax_off.rationale: Virtually no one uses Windows Fax. The service loads drivers and waits for fax lines — pure waste.
svc_fax_off.impact: One fewer starting service. Fax functionality removed (reversible).

svc_geolocation_off.name: Set Location Service (lfsvc) to Manual
svc_geolocation_off.description: Sets the Geolocation service to Manual.
svc_geolocation_off.rationale: lfsvc continuously sends location requests when active. Apps needing GPS/location start it themselves on demand.
svc_geolocation_off.impact: No permanent location tracking; Maps/weather apps can still start the service.

svc_wmp_network_off.name: Disable Windows Media Player Network Sharing
svc_wmp_network_off.rationale: Shares media over DLNA/UPnP on the home network — hardly anyone uses this through Windows itself. Unnecessary service + network listener.
svc_wmp_network_off.impact: No more DLNA streaming via Windows Media Player.

svc_maps_off.name: Disable Offline Maps Manager
svc_maps_off.rationale: Manages Windows offline map downloads. Anyone not using Windows Maps never needs this service.
svc_maps_off.impact: No automatic map updates; Windows Maps offline functionality unavailable.

svc_link_tracking_off.name: Disable Distributed Link Tracking Client
svc_link_tracking_off.rationale: Tracks NTFS links across network drives (legacy enterprise feature). On home systems it runs completely for nothing.
svc_link_tracking_off.impact: Broken shortcuts over network drives won't be auto-repaired — affects home users virtually never.

svc_wisvc_off.name: Disable Windows Insider Service
svc_wisvc_off.rationale: Only needed for the Windows Insider Program. Pure overhead for non-Insiders.
svc_wisvc_off.impact: No effect for non-Insiders; Insider Program can no longer be activated.

svc_diag_tracking_off.name: Disable Diagnostic Tracking Service
svc_diag_tracking_off.rationale: Collects runtime diagnostic traces for Microsoft Visual Studio and Windows Diagnostics. Unnecessary on non-developer systems.
svc_diag_tracking_off.impact: Visual Studio diagnostic tools stop working — irrelevant for normal gaming/desktop use.

wsearch_manual.name: Windows Search indexer: Manual start
wsearch_manual.description: Sets the WSearch service to Manual.
wsearch_manual.rationale: The indexer can cause sustained disk/CPU churn. Manual keeps search functional (slower, non-indexed) while stopping background indexing.
wsearch_manual.impact: Less background I/O; Start-menu file search becomes slower.

nagle_off.name: Disable Nagle's Algorithm
nagle_off.description: Sets TcpAckFrequency=1 and TCPNoDelay=1 on all network adapters, eliminating Nagle's packet-coalescing delay.
nagle_off.rationale: Nagle batches small TCP packets to reduce overhead — great for throughput, terrible for latency. Disabling it cuts ping spikes in competitive games.
nagle_off.impact: Lower ping variance and reduced input-to-server latency in online games.

tcp_timed_wait_low.name: Reduce TCP TIME_WAIT Delay
tcp_timed_wait_low.description: Sets TCPTimedWaitDelay to 30 seconds (default: 120). Frees up ports faster after connections close.
tcp_timed_wait_low.rationale: High-frequency game traffic opens/closes many short TCP connections. The default 120-second wait exhausts the ephemeral port range under load.
tcp_timed_wait_low.impact: Faster port recycling; eliminates 'unable to connect' errors during packet storms.

irp_stack_high.name: Increase Network IRP Stack Size
irp_stack_high.description: Sets IRPStackSize to 32 for the Server service (default: 15). Allows more simultaneous I/O request packets.
irp_stack_high.rationale: The default stack size can become a bottleneck when multiple games, voice chat, and streaming tools all hammer the network at once.
irp_stack_high.impact: Reduced network stalls under concurrent load (gaming + Discord + stream).

qos_bandwidth_free.name: Remove QoS Reserved Bandwidth
qos_bandwidth_free.description: Sets NonBestEffortLimit to 0, releasing the 20% bandwidth Windows reserves for QoS by default.
qos_bandwidth_free.rationale: Windows silently reserves 20% of your connection for background QoS traffic. On a 100 Mbit link that's 20 Mbit you never use in games.
qos_bandwidth_free.impact: Up to 20% more available bandwidth for game traffic.

tcp_autotuning_normal.name: Set TCP Auto-Tuning to Normal
tcp_autotuning_normal.description: Forces TCP receive window auto-tuning to 'normal' level for consistent throughput.
tcp_autotuning_normal.rationale: Auto-tuning can behave erratically behind some routers and cause latency spikes mid-game. 'Normal' gives a stable, well-tested tuning level.
tcp_autotuning_normal.impact: More consistent download speeds and lower jitter in online games.

net_rss_on.name: Enable Receive Side Scaling (RSS)
net_rss_on.description: Ensures RSS is enabled so network interrupts are spread across CPU cores instead of hammering core 0.
net_rss_on.rationale: Without RSS, all NIC interrupts fire on a single core. On a 16-core gaming rig that's a wasted opportunity — RSS distributes load and reduces per-core latency spikes.
net_rss_on.impact: Lower CPU-side network latency; fewer frame drops when download/upload peaks.

nv_power_max_perf.name: Maximum Performance (Disable PowerMizer)
nv_power_max_perf.description: Disables PowerMizer — NVIDIA otherwise aggressively lowers GPU clocks at idle and under load.
nv_power_max_perf.impact: GPU stays at maximum clocks permanently; power saving fully disabled. Not recommended for laptops on battery.

nv_dynamic_pstate_off.name: Disable Dynamic P-States
nv_dynamic_pstate_off.description: Stops dynamic switching between GPU performance states (P0–P8).
nv_dynamic_pstate_off.impact: Smoother frametimes — eliminates clock dips from mid-frame P-state transitions. Slightly higher idle power draw.

nv_fts_telemetry_off.name: Disable NVIDIA FTS Telemetry
nv_fts_telemetry_off.description: Sets three FTS telemetry flags to 0 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\NVIDIA Corporation\Global\FTS.
nv_fts_telemetry_off.impact: Stops NVIDIA usage telemetry upload. No effect on game performance.

nv_telemetry_svc_off.name: Disable NVIDIA Telemetry Container Service
nv_telemetry_svc_off.description: Sets NvContainerLocalSystem to Manual — prevents the NVIDIA telemetry container from auto-starting.
nv_telemetry_svc_off.impact: No NVIDIA telemetry process on boot. GeForce Experience and drivers continue to work normally.

nv_threaded_opt.name: Enable NVIDIA Threaded Optimization
nv_threaded_opt.description: Forces ThreadedOptimization globally — allows the NVIDIA driver to use multi-threading for OpenGL/D3D calls.
nv_threaded_opt.impact: Higher CPU-GPU throughput in many titles. Explicitly setting this prevents per-app overrides.

amd_ulps_off.name: Disable ULPS (Ultra Low Power State)
amd_ulps_off.description: Disables Ultra Low Power State — AMD GPUs otherwise enter a deep sleep that requires a wake-up cycle.
amd_ulps_off.impact: Eliminates the most common AMD micro-stutters on the first frame after idle. No measurable power increase during gaming.

amd_sclk_sleep_off.name: Disable Shader Clock Deep Sleep
amd_sclk_sleep_off.description: Prevents the shader clock from entering deep sleep during short inter-frame gaps.
amd_sclk_sleep_off.impact: More consistent shader performance; fewer latency spikes between frames.

amd_compute_preemption_off.name: Disable Compute Preemption
amd_compute_preemption_off.description: Disables preemptive interruption of compute workloads — reduces driver overhead.
amd_compute_preemption_off.impact: Lower driver latency for compute tasks; less frametime variance in DX12/Vulkan titles.

amd_dma_power_off.name: Disable DMA Power Gating
amd_dma_power_off.description: Keeps the GPU DMA engine permanently active instead of gating it during inactivity.
amd_dma_power_off.impact: Reduces latency on GPU driver DMA transfers; noticeable with fast texture uploads and asset streaming.

amd_rx_tex_cache.name: Enable Texture Cache Prefetch
amd_rx_tex_cache.description: Enables AMD Texture Cache Prefetching for RX GPUs.
amd_rx_tex_cache.impact: Higher texture throughput in open-world games with heavy asset streaming.

nv_prerender_limit_1.name: Max Pre-Rendered Frames = 1
nv_prerender_limit_1.description: Limits NVIDIA frame queue to 1 pre-rendered frame (default: 3). Reduces buffering between CPU and GPU — less input lag, especially at high FPS.
nv_prerender_limit_1.impact: 3–10 ms lower input lag. May cause minor stutter if CPU is the bottleneck.

nv_low_latency_ultra.name: NVIDIA Low Latency Ultra Mode
nv_low_latency_ultra.description: Sets NVIDIA frame submission to Ultra Low Latency (RTHM_MODE=2). GPU renders frames just-in-time — minimises the CPU→GPU pipeline delay.
nv_low_latency_ultra.impact: Lowest possible input lag in GPU-bound games. Best effect at 60–144 Hz with VSYNC off.

dx_shader_cache_unlimited.name: Unlimited DirectX Shader Cache
dx_shader_cache_unlimited.description: Removes the default 10 GB cap on the DirectX shader cache. Larger cache = fewer in-game shader compilation stalls (the "stutters" on first visit to an area).
dx_shader_cache_unlimited.impact: Eliminates shader compilation stutter in DX11/DX12 games after first run. Uses more disk space.

gpu_tweaks::integrated_only.title: No Discrete GPU Detected
gpu_tweaks::integrated_only.message: Most GPU tweaks require a dedicated graphics card. Only service-level tweaks (HAGS, MPO) may apply to integrated graphics.

gpu_tweaks::budget_gpu.title: Budget GPU
gpu_tweaks::budget_gpu.message: Budget GPUs are more sensitive to driver-level tweaks. Apply changes one at a time and test stability before applying more.

gpu_tweaks::older_arch_thermal.title: Older GPU Architecture — Thermal Risk
gpu_tweaks::older_arch_thermal.message: GTX 900/1000 and RX 400/500 series GPUs can run hot under P-state locks or forced max clocks. Monitor temps closely and ensure airflow is good.

gpu_tweaks::low_vram.title: Low VRAM (<4 GB)
gpu_tweaks::low_vram.message: No tweak increases VRAM capacity. In modern games, low VRAM causes stuttering even with optimal driver settings. Lower in-game texture quality.

gpu_tweaks::laptop_power_limit.title: Laptop GPU — Power Limited
gpu_tweaks::laptop_power_limit.message: Laptop GPUs are throttled by TDP limits set by the manufacturer. P-state and frequency tweaks may have limited effect and can increase heat.

perf_tweaks::very_low_cores.title: Very Low Core Count (≤2 Cores)
perf_tweaks::very_low_cores.message: Timer resolution improvements have diminishing returns on 2-core CPUs. Prioritize RAM and storage upgrades for the biggest gains.

perf_tweaks::laptop_timer.title: Laptop — Battery Impact
perf_tweaks::laptop_timer.message: High-resolution timers (0.5 ms) significantly increase power consumption. Use only while plugged in for gaming sessions.

perf_tweaks::low_ram_pagefile.title: Low RAM (<8 GB) — Keep Pagefile On
perf_tweaks::low_ram_pagefile.message: With less than 8 GB of RAM, disabling the pagefile risks crashes and out-of-memory errors. Recommended: set pagefile to System Managed.

perf_tweaks::wifi_network.title: Wi-Fi Connection Detected
perf_tweaks::wifi_network.message: Network tweaks are optimized for wired Ethernet. On Wi-Fi, adapter queue and interrupt tweaks may have less effect — a wired connection is strongly recommended for gaming.

game_booster::low_ram.title: Critical: Low RAM (<8 GB)
game_booster::low_ram.message: Every freed megabyte matters. Kill all non-essential background processes before launching your game. Consider a RAM upgrade for lasting improvement.

game_booster::no_discrete_gpu.title: No Discrete GPU
game_booster::no_discrete_gpu.message: Game Booster's GPU Performance Mode requires a dedicated GPU. CPU and process priority optimizations still apply.

game_booster::budget_cpu_priority.title: Budget CPU — Priority Boost Helps
game_booster::budget_cpu_priority.message: Setting your game to High priority has a larger impact on low core count CPUs. Make sure to kill all background apps first.

game_booster::laptop_perf.title: Laptop — Plug In Before Boosting
game_booster::laptop_perf.message: Ensure your laptop is plugged into power before boosting. Battery saver modes will cap CPU and GPU performance regardless of boost settings.

power_plan::laptop_battery_drain.title: Laptop — High Performance Drains Battery
power_plan::laptop_battery_drain.message: High Performance and Ultimate Performance plans disable CPU frequency scaling. This can reduce battery life by 30–60%. Use only while plugged in.

power_plan::budget_cpu_plan.title: Budget CPU — Power Plan is High Impact
power_plan::budget_cpu_plan.message: Low-end CPUs downclock aggressively on Balanced plan. Switching to High Performance ensures sustained base clocks — high impact for budget hardware.

power_plan::desktop_custom_plan_thermal_.title: Custom/Third-Party Power Plans Can Stack Heat With GPU Tweaks
power_plan::desktop_custom_plan_thermal_.message: Plans created by tools like Winhance unlock hidden Advanced Power Settings (Processor Boost Mode, Core Parking, min processor state) that pin the CPU at max clock 24/7 — the same thing 'Maximum Performance'/'Disable Dynamic P-States' GPU tweaks do for the GPU. On a card below high-tier in an OEM/compact case, running both chips pinned at max simultaneously raises shared case temps and can push the GPU into thermal throttling sooner, lowering FPS instead of raising it. If you're using a custom power plan AND GPU clock-lock tweaks together and saw an FPS regression, test switching back to Balanced first.

dashboard::hdd_only.title: No SSD Detected
dashboard::hdd_only.message: An SSD provides 5–10× faster Windows boot and load times. Upgrading to an SSD is the single highest-impact hardware improvement for most systems.

dashboard::low_ram.title: Low RAM (<8 GB)
dashboard::low_ram.message: 16 GB is the current gaming standard. With <8 GB, games regularly stutter due to RAM pressure. Optimizations help but won't overcome this hardware limit.

dashboard::no_discrete_gpu.title: Integrated Graphics Only
dashboard::no_discrete_gpu.message: A discrete GPU unlocks the full benefit of GPU tweaks and enables significantly higher in-game performance.

dashboard::wifi_latency.title: Wi-Fi Connection
dashboard::wifi_latency.message: Wired Ethernet provides lower ping, less jitter, and more consistent speeds for gaming. Consider switching if latency matters.

debloater::low_ram_debloat.title: RAM Below 16 GB — Debloating Recommended
debloater::low_ram_debloat.message: Removing bloatware frees background RAM and reduces idle CPU usage. High impact on systems with 8 GB or less.

latency::older_nvidia_dpc.title: Older NVIDIA GPU — Check Driver Version
latency::older_nvidia_dpc.message: GTX 900/1000-series GPUs can generate elevated DPC latency with outdated drivers. Ensure you're on the latest Game Ready Driver.

latency::wifi_dpc.title: Wi-Fi — High DPC Latency Risk
latency::wifi_dpc.message: Wi-Fi drivers are a common source of DPC latency spikes. If stalls appear in the Latency Analyzer, try disabling Wi-Fi power saving in Device Manager.

nv_power_max_perf::Laptop GPU — Power/Th.title: Laptop GPU — Power/Thermal Limited
nv_power_max_perf::Laptop GPU — Power/Th.message: Laptop GPUs are capped by a manufacturer TDP limit, not just clock speed. Forcing max clock cannot exceed that cap — it only adds heat, which throttles the CPU/GPU pair via shared cooling. Expect no FPS gain and possible loss.

nv_power_max_perf::Thermal Throttle Risk.title: Thermal Throttle Risk on This GPU
nv_power_max_perf::Thermal Throttle Risk.message: Locks your GPU at maximum clock permanently. On this card/cooling combo this usually raises baseline temperature until the GPU hits its thermal limit mid-game and throttles BELOW stock boost clocks — net result is often lower FPS, not higher. Only beneficial on high-end cards with strong cooling (large air/AIO). Test with a benchmark before/after; revert if FPS drops or temps exceed ~80°C.

nv_dynamic_pstate_off::Thermal Throttle.title: Thermal Throttle Risk on This GPU
nv_dynamic_pstate_off::Thermal Throttle.message: Functionally overlaps with 'Maximum Performance' — both force the GPU off its adaptive power curve. Combining them (or even just one, on this GPU tier) is the most common cause of FPS regressions reported on mid/budget cards. Apply only one at a time, monitor temps, revert if FPS drops.

hags_on::HAGS on Older GPU Architecture.title: HAGS on Older GPU Architecture
hags_on::HAGS on Older GPU Architecture.message: Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling was built for newer schedulers (NVIDIA 10-series+/AMD RDNA+ with current drivers) but real-world stutter/regression reports cluster on older architectures and outdated drivers. Benchmark before/after — revert if frametimes get worse.

amd_compute_preemption_off::Older AMD Ar.title: Older AMD Architecture
amd_compute_preemption_off::Older AMD Ar.message: Disabling compute preemption changes driver scheduling behavior that varies a lot by GPU generation. On older RX 400/500-series cards this can increase stutter instead of reducing it. Test before/after.

priority_separation::Aggressive Schedule.title: Aggressive Scheduler Bias on Low Core Count
priority_separation::Aggressive Schedule.message: Win32PrioritySeparation 0x26 gives the foreground app a hard 3:1 quanta bias. On 2-core/4-thread CPUs this can starve background services (audio, anti-cheat helpers, drivers) instead of helping — the opposite of the intended effect. Watch for audio crackle or input stutter after applying.

net_offload_disable::Budget CPU — Offloa.title: Budget CPU — Offload Disabling Increases CPU Load
net_offload_disable::Budget CPU — Offloa.message: Disabling NIC offloads (TCP checksum, LSO, RSS) moves that work from the network card back onto the CPU. On a low core-count CPU under load (game + voice chat + overlay) this can add enough overhead to cost more frame time than the latency it saves. Test before/after with a frame-time graph, not just ping.

mmcss_games_task::Maximum MMCSS Priority.title: Maximum MMCSS Priority on Low Core Count
mmcss_games_task::Maximum MMCSS Priority.message: GPU Priority 8 + CPU Priority 6 increases scheduling priority for any MMCSS-registered process. On budget CPUs (≤4 threads) this can cause contention with system threads and reduce, not improve, frame consistency.

power_throttling_off::Laptop — Heat & Ba.title: Laptop — Heat & Battery Impact
power_throttling_off::Laptop — Heat & Ba.message: Disabling EcoQoS power throttling stops Windows from parking background processes onto efficiency cores. On a laptop this raises sustained power draw and heat, which can trigger CPU thermal throttling under combined CPU+GPU load — possibly netting lower sustained FPS than with throttling enabled. Use only plugged in, monitor CPU temps.

pagefile_disable::Low RAM — Disabling Pa.title: Low RAM — Disabling Pagefile Risks Crashes
pagefile_disable::Low RAM — Disabling Pa.message: With under 8 GB RAM, the pagefile is not optional headroom — it's actively used. Disabling it on this system risks out-of-memory crashes and application instability. Keep at minimum 'System Managed'.

disable_paging_executive::Low RAM — Keep.title: Low RAM — Keep Kernel in RAM Not Recommended
disable_paging_executive::Low RAM — Keep.message: This tweak's own rationale only applies to systems with ≥8 GB RAM. On this system it pins kernel/driver pages permanently in RAM, removing headroom you don't have — increases the chance of paging thrash and crashes instead of reducing latency.

sysmain_off::HDD Detected — SysMain/Supe.title: HDD Detected — SysMain/Superfetch Helps You
sysmain_off::HDD Detected — SysMain/Supe.message: This tweak is intended for SSD systems. Your system drive is a mechanical HDD, where SysMain's prefetching actively hides slow seek/access times. Disabling it on an HDD-only system will make app launches and boot feel slower, not faster.

core_parking_off::Laptop — Battery & Hea.title: Laptop — Battery & Heat Impact
core_parking_off::Laptop — Battery & Hea.message: Keeps every CPU core powered at all times instead of parking idle cores. On a laptop this raises sustained power draw and heat — can shorten battery life noticeably and, under combined CPU+GPU load, contribute to thermal throttling that cancels out the latency gain this tweak aims for.

usb_suspend_off::Laptop — Battery Impact.title: Laptop — Battery Impact
usb_suspend_off::Laptop — Battery Impact.message: Keeps all USB devices fully powered, never suspended. Fine on AC power; on battery this is a measurable, continuous drain for the questionable benefit of avoiding mouse/headset micro-stutter most users never notice.

timer_resolution_min::Laptop — Battery &.title: Laptop — Battery & Heat Impact
timer_resolution_min::Laptop — Battery &.message: Forcing 0.5ms timer resolution wakes the CPU far more often than the Windows default (~15.6ms), increasing power draw and heat. On a laptop this measurably shortens battery life and can add to thermal load under sustained gaming.

power_plan_ultimate::Laptop — Ultimate P.title: Laptop — Ultimate Performance Disables Power Saving
power_plan_ultimate::Laptop — Ultimate P.message: Ultimate Performance disables nearly all power-saving parking/throttling. On a laptop this raises idle power draw and heat noticeably and shortens battery life — only use it on AC power.

power_plan_custom_unknown::Custom/Unknow.title: Custom/Unknown Power Plan
power_plan_custom_unknown::Custom/Unknow.message: This plan wasn't created by Windows or this app — its Advanced Power Settings (min processor state, Core Parking, Boost Mode) are unknown and may already be forcing max CPU clock 24/7. Combined with GPU clock-lock tweaks this can cause unexpected FPS regressions. Check Power Options → Change plan settings → Change advanced power settings before trusting it for gaming.
